data.body:
PASS 1 review note — T11 (Data-migration / backfill safety overlay): NOT-APPLICABLE / PASS.

The "single-source-of-truth snapshot-entry schema module" is a canonical shape CONTRACT used for validation/round-trip tests — it does not alter any persisted storage format and triggers no data backfill. Fixtures are captured-from-real-Jira test data (hermetic), not a production data migration. Story D (applier collapse) is a code-path refactor with behavioral-parity verification; Story E (sub-op telemetry + warn-first→hard-fail) adds in-memory/structured-outcome fields plus a runtime invariant — neither changes a persisted schema nor backfills rows. Snapshots are live in-memory Jira-REST structures fetched per-pass, not a persisted table being rewritten. Per the overlay's ANTI-FP ("not-applicable for non-persisted/in-memory changes"), T11 raises no findings.
